regime_reason: S&P 500 (7,543) and Nasdaq (26,207, +1.3% on SK Hynix's Nasdaq debut and a chip-led rally) are both near record highs while VIX fell 6.3% to 15.84, a clean risk-on read, though June CPI (Tue 7/14, prior print hot at 4.2% YoY) ahead of the July 28-29 FOMC is a real event risk to watch.

watchlist.note: Transocean: $1B+ Equinor Cat-D rig backlog (7/8) plus ~$185M Norway/Australia backlog add; director Chad Deaton bought $173k on 7/2; earnings 8/5. Valaris merger faces DOJ second-request delay.

Absci: Leerink/Guggenheim/BTIG initiated bullish coverage this week citing ABS-201 as a multi-billion-dollar hair-loss/endometriosis opportunity, proof-of-concept data due 2H26; stock +1.9% today, still riding the 6/24-7/2 PT-hike wave.

Hudson Technologies: DLA bridge-modification extends its refrigerant supply contract to 11/29/26 (with options to 5/29/27); 10%-owner Hartree Partners' insider buying spree ran through 7/7 (~$4.5M total).

notes: Held positions: AVO -- director Jay Pack bought $484k on 6/30, GlobalHarvest's insider streak continues, no negative news (analyst PT avg $17.50 vs ~$13.46). LRMR -- filed the first BLA module for nomlabofusp accelerated approval 6/29 with positive long-term open-label data; Baird's PT cut to $5 (noted yesterday) is the only incremental negative to watch. WRAP -- new buy today after a +48% weekly run on an ATF ruling reclassifying BolaWrap plus the WrapShield/Frenel platform launch and a $110k director buy (7/8); real catalysts but the move is extended, so watch for a pullback. New avoid adds: BRLS (Borealis Foods, below Nasdaq's $35M market-value listing standard, cured by 12/29/26 or faces delisting), CERO and GTI (named in Nasdaq's 7/10 delisting notice batch), JZXN (Jiuzi Holdings +102% on an unsigned cooperation MOU with volume ~90x float -- textbook hype pump, no real catalyst).
